prediction:
To compute the determinant of the given 3×3 matrix:

$$
\begin{pmatrix}
-\frac{4}{3} & 4 & -\frac{11}{6} \\
-\frac{13}{3} & \frac{23}{6} & -\frac{25}{6} \\
\frac{5}{3} & -\frac{2}{3} & -\frac{5}{3}
\end{pmatrix}
$$

we apply the standard cofactor expansion along the first row. The determinant of a 3×3 matrix is given by:

$$
\text{det} = a(ei - fh) - b(di - fg) + c(dh - eg)
$$

Assigning the elements:

- $ a = -\frac{4}{3},\ b = 4,\ c = -\frac{11}{6} $
- $ d = -\frac{13}{3},\ e = \frac{23}{6},\ f = -\frac{25}{6} $
- $ g = \frac{5}{3},\ h = -\frac{2}{3},\ i = -\frac{5}{3} $

---

### Step 1: Compute the first term

$$
a(ei - fh) = -\frac{4}{3} \left( \frac{23}{6} \cdot -\frac{5}{3} - \left(-\frac{25}{6} \cdot -\frac{2}{3} \right) \right)
$$

$$
= -\frac{4}{3} \left( -\frac{115}{18} - \frac{50}{18} \right) = -\frac{4}{3} \cdot \left( -\frac{165}{18} \right) = \frac{4 \cdot 55}{3 \cdot 6} = \frac{220}{18} = \frac{110}{9}
$$

---

### Step 2: Compute the second term

$$
-b(di - fg) = -4 \left( -\frac{13}{3} \cdot -\frac{5}{3} - \left(-\frac{25}{6} \cdot \frac{5}{3} \right) \right)
$$

$$
= -4 \left( \frac{65}{9} + \frac{125}{18} \right) = -4 \cdot \left( \frac{130 + 125}{18} \right) = -4 \cdot \frac{255}{18} = -\frac{1020}{18} = -\frac{170}{3}
$$

---

### Step 3: Compute the third term

$$
c(dh - eg) = -\frac{11}{6} \left( -\frac{13}{3} \cdot -\frac{2}{3} - \frac{23}{6} \cdot \frac{5}{3} \right)
$$

$$
= -\frac{11}{6} \left( \frac{26}{9} - \frac{115}{18} \right) = -\frac{11}{6} \cdot \left( \frac{52 - 115}{18} \right) = -\frac{11}{6} \cdot \left( -\frac{63}{18} \right)
$$

$$
= \frac{11 \cdot 63}{6 \cdot 18} = \frac{693}{108} = \frac{77}{12}
$$

---

### Step 4: Add all three terms

Convert all to a common denominator of 36:

- $ \frac{110}{9} = \frac{440}{36} $
- $ -\frac{170}{3} = -\frac{2040}{36} $
- $ \frac{77}{12} = \frac{231}{36} $

$$
\text{det} = \frac{440 - 2040 + 231}{36} = \frac{-1369}{36}
$$

---

### Final Answer

$$
\boxed{-\dfrac{1369}{36}}
$$
